qas.a: What plan outlines the training individuals in this AFS should receive to support their professional development and defines the competencies necessary to progress throughout their careers?
Who will develop and revise formal resident, non-resident, field and exportable training based upon requirements established by the users and documented in Part II of the CFETP?
Who will ensure training programs complement the CFETP?
Graduation from UCT meets what career field training standard?

What specialty Plans, organizes, directs and executes cyberspace and information operations such as, Defensive Cyber Operations (DCO), Offensive Cyber Operations (OCO), Department of Defense (DoD) Information Network (DoDIN) Operations and Mission Assurance for Air Force weapons systems and platforms?
For award of AFSC 17D3 and 17S3, officers must meet what training standard identified by their Commander, MAJCOM and Desired Operational Capability Statements?
Officer must be appointed to a staff position at the MAJCOM, Numbered Air Force, Field Operating Agency, Headquarters Air Staff, or Combatant Command to achieve this Staff Level.

qas.q: The Cyberspace Operations Officer Career Path Pyramid
qas.a: What tool is used as a planning guide by commanders, supervisors, and individuals in conjunction with other planning tools, to include AFI 36-2110, and senior cyberspace operations officer advice.

What tool provides AF functional and CFMs the data necessary to monitor and develop the force to the appropriate breadth and depth of experience required for the health of the career field. and allows the AFPC assignment team and the Cyberspace Operations Development Team (DT) to quickly identify candidates for positions requiring specific experiences and certifications.
All Cyber Operations Officers will attain and possess a current Information Assurance Management certification in accordance?

qas.q: The Air Force Institute of Technology (AFIT) Graduate School of Engineering and Management
qas.a: Located at Wright-Patterson AFB, OH, what institution offers cyberspace related courses to include Graduate programs in Cyber Operations, Computer Engineering, Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, and Software Engineering.
What course provides a comprehensive macro view of the contingency and crisis action planning processes from both joint and Air Force perspectives.

qas.q: The Joint Command, Control, Communications, and Computers (C4) Planners Course
qas.a: What course has the mission to educate C4 Planners in Doctrinal C4 concepts in the Joint, Interagency, and Coalition environments.
